creator: salvadore@freebsd.org
text:
In the following text some # characters have disappeared: The format of the kernel configuration file is simple. Each line contains a keyword that represents a device or subsystem, an argument, and a brief description. Any text after a is considered a comment and ignored. To remove kernel support for a device or subsystem, put a at the beginning of the line representing that device or subsystem. Do not add or remove a # for any line that you do not understand. Looking at the page source, this code can be found, where the characters are indeed missing:

The format of the kernel configuration file is simple. Each line contains a keyword that represents a device or subsystem, an argument, and a brief description. Any text after a is considered a comment and ignored. To remove kernel support for a device or subsystem, put a at the beginning of the line representing that device or subsystem. Do not add or remove a # for any line that you do not understand.

However, looking at /documentation/content/en/books/handbook/kernelconfig/_index.adoc the characters seem to be present: The format of the kernel configuration file is simple. Each line contains a keyword that represents a device or subsystem, an argument, and a brief description. Any text after a `#` is considered a comment and ignored. To remove kernel support for a device or subsystem, put a `#` at the beginning of the line representing that device or subsystem. Do not add or remove a `#` for any line that you do not understand.
